message:
I had to laugh at myself last week. My kid asked for a snack during a surprise power outage, and instead of panic, I just opened the kitchen drawer where I'd stashed a little emergency kit three months ago—granola bars, some dried fruit, a flashlight. Five minutes of prep had literally saved me from standing there feeling helpless.

It got me thinking: preparedness doesn't have to be this big overwhelming thing. Sometimes it's just one drawer. One bag by the door. One folder with important papers. Those little wins—the ones you almost forget about—they're the ones that actually show up when you need them.

What's one small thing you've prepped that's already come in handy for you?
